evals.prompt: /cyoda:build — add an Order entity with draft and submitted states
evals.expected_output: Reads config, inspects existing models, generates workflow JSON, shows it for confirmation, and stops awaiting user approval — does not register without confirmation

assertions.text: Reads active profile from ~/.config/cyoda/cyoda-plugin-config.json for endpoint
Inspects existing entity models on the instance
Asks minimum necessary clarifying questions before generating — may proceed directly without asking when entity name, states, and transitions are fully specified in the request
Shows generated workflow JSON for user confirmation before registering
Does NOT register without showing config for confirmation first

evals.prompt: /cyoda:build — add a cancel transition to the Order entity
evals.expected_output: Inspects existing Order entity, shows current states/transitions, asks which state 'cancel' transitions from, generates updated config, shows for confirmation, registers

assertions.text: Inspects existing Order entity models
Shows current states and transitions before proposing changes
Asks which state 'cancel' transitions from
Shows updated config for confirmation before registering
Does NOT create a brand new entity instead of modifying existing

evals.expected_output: Shows PRODUCTION warning at start, generates config normally, requires explicit 'confirm' before registering — does not block the full build loop

assertions.text: Shows PRODUCTION warning at the start of the skill
Generates config and shows for confirmation as normal
Requires user to type 'confirm' before registering to production
Does NOT register to production without explicit 'confirm'
Does NOT block the entire build loop — only adds the confirmation step

evals.prompt: /cyoda:build — register a new ChatRoom entity with a simple workflow
evals.expected_output: Invokes cyoda:docs for any endpoint not listed in the skill, executes the correct sample-entity-then-workflow-import sequence, and does not guess or retry alternate URLs

assertions.text: Invokes cyoda:docs before calling any endpoint not explicitly listed in the skill
Does NOT construct or guess API URLs — uses only endpoints listed in the skill or confirmed via cyoda:docs
Executes registration in the correct sequence: POST sample entity to /api/entity/JSON/{entity}/{version} first, then POST workflow to /api/model/{entity}/{version}/workflow/import
Does NOT retry failed endpoints with guessed variants — invokes cyoda:docs on 4xx before retrying

evals.prompt: /cyoda:build — register a new Order entity with draft and submitted states on a fresh instance
evals.expected_output: Posts a sample entity to create the model in discover mode before importing the workflow — does not call workflow/import as the first API call

assertions.text: POSTs a sample entity to /api/entity/JSON/{entity}/1 before calling /api/model/{entity}/1/workflow/import
Does NOT call /api/model/{entity}/1/workflow/import as the first API call on a fresh instance
Does NOT lock the schema — stays in discover mode after workflow import

evals.prompt: /cyoda:build — I want to add a ChatRoom entity and a Message entity
evals.expected_output: Builds both entities in discover mode without suggesting or performing schema locking — mentions discover mode stays active during development

assertions.text: Does NOT suggest locking the schema during a normal development build session
Does NOT execute a schema lock operation
Mentions that schema stays in discover mode during development

evals.prompt: /cyoda:build — export the ChatRoom model schema so I can inspect it
evals.expected_output: Invokes cyoda:docs to look up the export endpoint before making any curl call — does not guess the URL

assertions.text: Invokes cyoda:docs to look up the correct export endpoint path
Does NOT attempt curl calls with guessed URL variants (e.g. /api/model/export/JSON/SIMPLE_VIEW/... or /api/model/export/JSON/...)

evals.prompt: /cyoda:build — add a Message entity with a sent state
evals.expected_output: When a registration call returns 4xx, invokes cyoda:docs rather than retrying with guessed alternate paths

assertions.text: On a 4xx response, invokes cyoda:docs to look up the correct endpoint before retrying
Does NOT retry the call with guessed alternate URL variants after a 4xx
Does NOT search the filesystem for the cyoda binary when cyoda is not in PATH

evals.prompt: /cyoda:build — add an archived state to the ChatRoom entity
evals.expected_output: Detects ChatRoom already exists at version 1 and asks whether to update the existing version or create version 2 before generating any config

assertions.text: Reads the current model version from the GET /api/model response
Asks user whether to update existing version 1 or create a new version 2
Does NOT silently assume version 1 and proceed without surfacing the decision
Uses the version chosen by the user in all subsequent API calls

evals.prompt: /cyoda:build — yes, the Order entity config looks right, please register it
evals.expected_output: Executes the registration POST calls (sample entity then workflow import), shows the API response, re-inspects the model state, reports completion, and suggests running /cyoda:test

assertions.text: Attempts POST to /api/entity/JSON/Order/{version} with a sample payload before the workflow import
Attempts POST to /api/model/Order/{version}/workflow/import with the workflow config
Shows the API response (success or failure) to the user rather than silently continuing
Re-inspects the running instance (GET /api/model) after registration to show what changed
Offers to run /cyoda:test or suggests it as the next step

evals.prompt: /cyoda:build — add an Order entity with draft and submitted states
evals.expected_output: Detects pom.xml, saves workflow JSON to src/main/resources/workflow/order-v1-workflow.json and sample data to src/main/resources/data/order-v1-sample.json before registering via API

assertions.text: Detects Java project by presence of pom.xml
Saves workflow JSON to src/main/resources/workflow/order-v1-workflow.json
Saves sample entity JSON to src/main/resources/data/order-v1-sample.json
File names follow the {entity}-v{version}-{type} pattern
Tells the user the saved file path(s) before proceeding to register

evals.prompt: /cyoda:build — add an Invoice entity with a pending state
evals.expected_output: Finds no build file but discovers an existing workflow/ directory, mirrors that layout for the new entity, uses versioned naming

assertions.text: Finds no pom.xml, package.json, or Python build file in the project root
Scans and finds the existing workflow/ directory
Saves the new invoice workflow to the same workflow/ directory rather than creating a new path
Writes the file as workflow/invoice-v1-workflow.json

evals.prompt: /cyoda:build — add a Product entity and a Category entity both with active and archived states
evals.expected_output: Generates and saves separate versioned files for each entity in the same typed folders; does not mix entity content into a single file

assertions.text: Writes a separate workflow file for each entity: src/workflows/product-v1-workflow.json and src/workflows/category-v1-workflow.json
Does NOT combine both entities into a single workflow file
Uses src/workflows/ for workflow JSON and src/data/ for sample data (TypeScript project detected via package.json + tsconfig.json)

evals.expected_output: Detects 401 on GET /api/model, invokes cyoda:auth to refresh, retries the model inspection once

assertions.text: Invokes cyoda:auth when any API call returns 401 or 403
Retries the failed call exactly once after auth refresh
Does NOT retry on 404 or 5xx — only on 401/403
